item.ptxt: If any DTC other than C1074, C1075 is detected, sensor calibration cannot be completed. Repair the cause of the DTC first.
ESP® hydraulic unit / control module assembly has reference values for master cylinder pressure sensor and yaw rate / G sensor stored. Also steering angle sensor has its own reference point stored.

Traction control and stability control are performed according to the variation of these sensors from their reference points. If a reference value is lost or shifted due to following maintenance, calibration is needed.

entry.ptxt: Replace ESP® hydraulic unit / control module assembly (C1074)
Replace ESP® hydraulic unit / control module assembly

item.ptxt: All sensors are calibrated at once when calibration is performed.

s1.ptxt: Park vehicle on level surface, apply parking brake, stop engine with ignition “ON”, set steering wheel in straight-ahead position and release brake pedal.

attention3.ptxt: Keep condition of Step 4) to calibrate sensor correctly until sensor calibration is completed.

s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1) with ignition “OFF”.
Set ignition “ON” and confirm that no DTC is detected except C1074 and C1075. If any DTC is detected, repair or replace trouble area.
Select “Sensor Calibration” under “Utility” of SUZUKI scan tool and calibrate sensor with ignition “ON”. Refer to scan tool operator’s manual for further details.
After completing the calibration, check DTC(s) is not stored in ESP® hydraulic unit / control module assembly.
Confirm that ignition is “OFF” and then disconnect SUZUKI scan tool from DLC.
